heading: Issuance and treatment of payment stablecoins
label: 5902 Issuance and treatment of payment stablecoins

children.heading: Limitation on issuers
children.label: a Limitation on issuers
children.text: It shall be unlawful for any person other than a permitted payment stablecoin issuer to issue a payment stablecoin in the United States.

children.heading: Prohibition on offers or sales
children.label: b Prohibition on offers or sales

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (c) and , beginning on the date that is 3 years after , it shall be unlawful for a digital asset service provider to offer or sell a payment stablecoin to a person in the United States, unless the payment stablecoin is issued by a permitted payment stablecoin issuer.section 5916 of this titleJuly 18, 2025

children.heading: Foreign payment stablecoin issuers
children.label: 2 Foreign payment stablecoin issuers
children.text: It shall be unlawful for any digital asset service provider to offer, sell, or otherwise make available in the United States a payment stablecoin issued by a foreign payment stablecoin issuer unless the foreign payment stablecoin issuer has the technological capability to comply, and will comply, with the terms of any lawful order and any reciprocal arrangement pursuant to .section 5916 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: consistent with the purposes of the chapter;1So in original. Probably should be “this”.1

children.label: C
children.text: apply to a de minimis volume of transactions, as determined by the Secretary of the Treasury.2So in original.2

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the Secretary of the Treasury determines that unusual and exigent circumstances exist, the Secretary may provide limited safe harbors from subsection (a).

children.heading: Justification
children.label: B Justification
children.text: Prior to issuing a limited safe harbor under this paragraph, the Secretary of the Treasury shall submit to the chairs and ranking members of the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives a justification for the determination of the unusual and exigent circumstances, which may be contained in a classified annex, as applicable.

children.heading: Rulemaking
children.label: d Rulemaking
children.text: Consistent with , the Secretary of the Treasury shall issue regulations to implement this section, including regulations to define terms.section 5913 of this title

children.heading: Extraterritorial effect
children.label: e Extraterritorial effect
children.text: This section is intended to have extraterritorial effect if conduct involves the offer or sale of a payment stablecoin to a person located in the United States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Whoever knowingly participates in a violation of subsection (a) shall be fined not more than $1,000,000 for each such violation, imprisoned for not more than 5 years, or both.

children.heading: Referral to Attorney General
children.label: 2 Referral to Attorney General
children.text: If a primary Federal payment stablecoin regulator has reason to believe that any person has knowingly violated subsection (a), the primary Federal payment stablecoin regulator may refer the matter to the Attorney General.
treated as cash or as a cash equivalent for accounting purposes;
eligible as cash or as a cash equivalent margin and collateral for futures commission merchants, derivative clearing organizations, broker-dealers, registered clearing agencies, and swap dealers; or
acceptable as a settlement asset to facilitate wholesale payments between banking organizations or by a payment infrastructure to facilitate exchange and settlement among banking organizations.

children.label: A
children.text: the direct transfer of digital assets between 2 individuals acting on their own behalf and for their own lawful purposes, without the involvement of an intermediary;

children.label: B
children.text: to any transaction involving the receipt of digital assets by an individual between an account owned by the individual in the United States and an account owned by the individual abroad that are offered by the same parent company; or3So in original. The word “to” probably should not appear.3

children.label: C
children.text: to any transaction by means of a software or hardware wallet that facilitates an individual’s own custody of digital assets.3

children.heading: Treasury authority
children.label: 2 Treasury authority
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall alter the existing authority of the Secretary of the Treasury to block, restrict, or limit transactions involving payment stablecoins that reference or are denominated in United States dollars that are subject to the jurisdiction of the United States.
